concentration.headline: Highly distributed urban network
concentration.body: No single city overwhelms China's urban landscape. Guangzhou holds 2.0% of tracked urban population across 1714 cities — characteristic of federal or historically polycentric countries with multiple strong economic centers.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: United States, Germany, Switzerland

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Guangzhou & Dongguan
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Guangzhou and Dongguan (52 km apart, combined population 37,586,000). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 73 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: China presents a classic polycentric structure: no primate city, with Guangzhou holding just 2.0% of tracked urban population. Gravity is strongest within a tight sub-regional cluster (Guangzhou → Dongguan, 73 km average separation) rather than radiating from a single capital — typical of federal economies with distributed industrial heritage.
